basics.name: Miguel Claramunt
basics.label: Data Scientist
basics.summary: Data Scientist with a strong foundation in AI data science, and analytics, combining academic and industry experience to deliver scalable solutions. Specialized in Natural Language Processing, Large Language Models, and High-Performance Computing.

work.name: Language Technologies Laboratory, Barcelona Supercomputing Center
work.summary: Engineered state-of-the-art machine translation systems, applying deep learning techniques to enhance multilingual NLP pipelines. Collaborated in cutting-edge research, optimizing translation pipelines and advancing state-of-the-art computational linguistics solutions for large-scale applications.

work.name: Leeos Merch
work.summary: Developed Customer Service solutions for four online merchandising stores of top Spanish-speaking content creators on YouTube and Twitch.

work.name: SDG Group
work.summary: Developed BI solutions for CaixaBank, enabling strategic and organizational decision-making. Maintained the 'Mis Ventas' Qlik Sense app serving 40K+ daily users with real-time updates, engineered ETL pipelines processing 500K+ records monthly, and streamlined workflows to reduce costs.

work.name: Intelligent Data Analysis Laboratory (IDAL), Universitat de València

awards.title: City of Valencia Data Visualization Award
awards.awarder: Chair of Gamification and Open Government of València (Càtedra de Ludificació i Govern Obert a la Ciutat de València)
awards.name: Albert Einstein
awards.label: Scientist
awards.summary: A German-born theoretical physicist, widely ranked among the greatest and most influential scientists of all time

work.name: Institute for Advanced Study, Princeton University
work.summary: Teaching at Palmer Physical Laboratory (now 302 Frist Campus Center). While not a professor at Princeton, I associated with the physics professors and continued to give lectures on campus.

volunteer.summary: Led a 9-monitor team for 2 years, organizing weekly activities for up to 20 children. Coordinated an 11-day summer camp with a 14-member team, overseeing activities for 50+ children.
Lead organizer for the New York City branch of the People's Climate March, the largest climate march in history.
highlights: Awarded 'Climate Hero' award by Greenpeace for my efforts organizing the march.
Men of the year 2014 by Time magazine

awards.title: Nobel Prize in Physics
awards.summary: The Nobel Prizes are five separate prizes that, according to Alfred Nobel's will of 1895, are awarded to 'those who, during the preceding year, have conferred the greatest benefit to humankind.'

publications.name: From SALAMANDRA to SALAMANDRATA: BSC Submission for WMT25 General Machine Translation Shared Task
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the Tenth Conference on Machine Translation

presentations.name: Detecting and Mitigating Bias in AI Systems: Webcafé with Javi García Gilabert and Miguel Claramunt
Zur Elektrodynamik bewegter Körper
presentations.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

presentations.name: Über einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt
presentations.summary: In the second paper, he applied the quantum theory to light to explain the photoelectric effect. In particular, he used the idea of light quanta (photons) to explain experimental results, but stressed the importance of the experimental results. The importance of his work on the photoelectric effect earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

presentations.name: Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitätstheorie
presentations.summary: The publication of the theory of general relativity made him internationally famous. He was professor of physics at the universities of Zurich (1909–1911) and Prague (1911–1912), before he returned to ETH Zurich (1912–1914).

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
